_meta.doc: Sidecar metadata for data/n5_kanji_whitelist.json. The whitelist itself is a flat top-level JSON array of 106 single-kanji strings — that shape is intentional because furigana.js, audit_dokkai_kanji_scope.py, audit_teacher_review.py, and check_content_integrity.py all parse it as `set(json.loads(...))`. Adding inline metadata would break those callers, so docs live here.
_meta.source: Curated from KnowledgeBank/kanji_n5.md by tools/build_data.py. The list mirrors the official JEES JLPT N5 kanji syllabus.
_meta.ordering_convention: Author-curated (roughly first-encountered-in-lesson-order, not strictly sorted by stroke count or Unicode). The order is stable across builds; new additions go at the end.
_meta.exceptions: data/dokkai_kanji_exception.json lists kanji that may appear in dokkai passages and (since 2026-05-08) in moji/goi/bunpou paper distractors despite NOT being in the whitelist. JA-13 + JA-28 + JA-40 enforce the surface-specific rules.
see_also: data/kanji.json (full per-kanji records with examples + stroke-order SVG paths)
